channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Houston Rockets
channel.description: Houston Rockets trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Yet when VanVleet addressed the room in Las Vegas, his ‚Ä¶
item.description: Soon after he was elected president of the National Basketball Players Association, Fred VanVleet, the stocky point guard of the Houston Rockets, walked to the front of a Las Vegas ballroom. It was July 2025, a few weeks removed from the Oklahoma City Thunder winning the NBA Finals, yet the ensuing season was already under way. Rookies had been drafted. Developmental prospects had come to Vegas for exhibition games. And with them had arrived a basketball establishment‚Äîcoaches, executives, team o

item.title: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA‚Ä¨: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in the play-by-play era (1997-98) to record 300+ fourth quarter PTS in the postseason before turning 25! Joining: Kobe Bryant (625 PTS) LeBron James (493 PTS) Jayson Tatum (388 PTS) Kevin Durant (388 PTS) Dwyane Wade (357 PTS)

item.title: Tonight was Anthony Edwards‚Äô 18th postseason game with ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA‚Ä¨: Tonight was Anthony Edwards‚Äô 18th postseason game with 30+ points, tying Dwyane Wade for the fourth-most by a player before turning 25. Trailing only... LeBron James (30) Kobe Bryant (26) Kevin Durant (22)

item.title: Yet when VanVleet addressed the room in Las Vegas, his ‚Ä¶
item.description: Soon after he was elected president of the National Basketball Players Association, Fred VanVleet, the stocky point guard of the Houston Rockets, walked to the front of a Las Vegas ballroom. It was July 2025, a few weeks removed from the Oklahoma City Thunder winning the NBA Finals, yet the ensuing season was already under way. Rookies had been drafted. Developmental prospects had come to Vegas for exhibition games. And with them had arrived a basketball establishment‚Äîcoaches, executives, team o

item.title: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA‚Ä¨: Anthony Edwards (302 PTS) becomes the 6th player in the play-by-play era (1997-98) to record 300+ fourth quarter PTS in the postseason before turning 25! Joining: Kobe Bryant (625 PTS) LeBron James (493 PTS) Jayson Tatum (388 PTS) Kevin Durant (388 PTS) Dwyane Wade (357 PTS)

item.title: Tonight was Anthony Edwards‚Äô 18th postseason game with ‚Ä¶
item.description: ‚Ä™NBA‚Ä¨: Tonight was Anthony Edwards‚Äô 18th postseason game with 30+ points, tying Dwyane Wade for the fourth-most by a player before turning 25. Trailing only... LeBron James (30) Kobe Bryant (26) Kevin Durant (22)

item.title: NBPA releases statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: The NBPA: Statement from NBPA Executive Director Andre Iguodala and NBPA President Fred VanVleet on the tragic passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: Dave Rennie maintains he hasn‚Äôt yet decided who will ‚Ä¶
item.description: Dave Rennie maintains he hasn‚Äôt yet decided who will captain the All Blacks, but he‚Äôs provided an insight into the process to pick his man. Back in New Zealand while his Japanese team on a bye, the new All Blacks coach revealed Kiwi NBA player Steven Adams had even been involved in the process. Adams, who plays for the Houston Rockets, was invited to a two-hour meeting with All Blacks greats Richie McCaw, Dan Carter and Keven Mealamu, and came armed with answers to questions sent by mental skill

item.title: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted ‚Ä¶
item.description: One source with knowledge of the situation insisted Tuesday that the widely criticized trade of McCain to Oklahoma City for a 2026 first-round pick via Houston and three second-round picks was not the proverbial last straw. Ownership very much approved the McCain deal and sources say that Sixers leadership feels quite confident in its post-Morey scouting ability to ensure that a quality player is selected at No. 22 overall next month.

item.title: It‚Äôs hard to gauge whether the Rockets got the better ‚Ä¶
item.description: How do you think the Kevin Durant trade played out for the Rockets? Was it worth losing players such as Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks? Metta World Peace: I think it‚Äôs played out the same way for both the Rockets and the Phoenix Suns. It was quite an even trade. It‚Äôs tough for the Rockets because Durant missed Game 1 with a knee injury, then injured his ankle in Game 2, causing him to miss more time. That hurt them a lot. I‚Äôve been injured in the playoffs, so I know how it feels. I was supposed t

item.title: The WNBA and NBA board of governors unanimously ‚Ä¶
item.description: The WNBA and NBA board of governors unanimously approved the sale and relocation of the Connecticut Sun to Houston Rockets owner Tilman Fertitta, the league announced Wednesday. The two sides had reached an agreement to sell the team for $300 million in March, according to a person familiar with the decision. The person spoke then on condition of anonymity because of the sensitive nature of the sale. The two sides had to wait for approval from the board of governors to make it official.

item.title: L2M report says there were zero incorrect calls late in Cavaliers-Pistons game, including contact between Jarrett Allen, Ausar Thompson
item.description: Danny Cunningham: The NBA‚Äôs Last 2 Minute Report says that there were zero incorrect calls in the final two minutes of regulation and overtime in last night‚Äôs #Cavs win over the Pistons.

item.title: ‚ÄúAllen and Thompson legally step to the same spot ‚Ä¶
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from #Cavs-#Pistons Game 5 shows no missed calls. The review of the final play: ‚ÄúAllen and Thompson legally step to the same spot while pursuing the loose ball [before either player has possession] & both lose their balance from the marginal contact‚Äù

item.title: He would practice those things and work on them, even ‚Ä¶
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on Harden, who he coached for four years in Houston: ‚ÄúHe would foul bait me. We would play one on one. He would practice those things and work on them, even just playing one on one versus me. He has a great understanding of the rules and how to manipulate them in his favor.‚Äù
